realize.stub_reason: unsupported canonical call `blake3::Hasher::new`; no Python shim matched `call:new(blake3::Hasher::new, [])`

claim: v0 establishes the end-to-end pipeline (sugar-walk-emit -> sugar-realize-python-core) for libsugar's first function. The Python output is the literal stdout of the realize-kit. The realize-kit emits an honest NotImplementedError stub because the term's first operation (call:new on blake3::Hasher::new) has no body-template entry in python-canonical-bodies-libsugar.json. This is a vendor-shim gap, not a realizer term-shape gap.
what_v1_needs: Either (a) author body-template entries for blake3, hex, and String FFI ops in python-canonical-bodies-libsugar.json (per-library shim work, no substrate), OR (b) run the vendor-absorption flow (issue #985) to auto-mint concept hubs + body-template entries for the blake3 vendor library at its API edge.
out_of_scope_tonight: Substrate changes: new concept hubs, lifter effect-tracking for statement-level method calls, RefusalMemento variants. Per the bootstrap arc rules, per-language kit work cannot mint new concept-shape specs.
